capabilities: Persist domain events as immutable records to facilitate reliable state reconstruction and auditability in event-driven architectures.

procedure.action: Specify domain events with explicit schemas using Avro or Protobuf to ensure consistency and forward compatibility.

Store events in an append-only log using a distributed event store like Apache Kafka or Amazon Kinesis using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Invoke snapshotting every 1000 events using a tool like Akka Persistence to optimize state reconstruction following the specified pattern; write a unit test covering the primary code path; assert test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete.

Invoke a message broker like RabbitMQ to publish events to interested subscribers for real-time processing by invoking the target API with the specified parameters; assert response_status < 400 and response_time_ms < 5000; log request_id and response_code.
Set up a monitoring dashboard with Grafana to track event processing latency and alert if it exceeds 200ms.

summary: Specialize event sourcing by persisting immutable domain events with domain-specific constraints, ensuring reliable state reconstruction and auditability.
title: Persist Immutable Domain Events for Event Sourcing

failureConditions: State reconstruction becomes slow due to excessive event replay.
Event schema changes break compatibility, causing processing failures.
Loss of event data due to improper log management.
metrics: test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete
successConditions: Verify event log integrity by checking for missing or duplicated events; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Ensure event processing latency remains under 200ms in 95% of cases; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Validate schema compatibility with backward and forward tests; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
